paper_title: Galactica: A Large Language Model for Science

raw_extraction.innovation: Specialized tokenization for scientific modalities (e.g., <work> for step-by-step reasoning, [START_REF]/[END_REF] for citations, [START_SMILES]/[END_SMILES] for chemical structures) combined with a highly curated scientific corpus to enable domain-specific knowledge absorption.
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Curated corpus of 106 billion tokens including 48M papers (88B tokens), code (7B tokens), reference material (7B tokens), knowledge bases (2B tokens), filtered Common Crawl (1B tokens), prompts (0.4B tokens), and other sources (0.2B tokens)
raw_extraction.application: Scientific literature review, equation solving, citation prediction, drug discovery (SMILES), protein sequence annotation, medical Q&A (PubMedQA, MedMCQA), mathematical reasoning (MMLU, MATH), and multi-modal scientific data synthesis
raw_extraction.research_problem: Organizing, retrieving, and reasoning over scientific knowledge using large language models
raw_extraction.pretraining_task: Next-token prediction (with specialized tokens for citations, reasoning steps, SMILES, amino acids, DNA, LaTeX)
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Instruction tuning, knowledge-intensive question answering, citation prediction, molecular structure generation, and drug discovery tasks
raw_extraction.extension: <work> token enables explicit step-by-step reasoning simulation, addressing limitations of chain-of-thought prompting by capturing intermediate computational steps internally rather than relying solely on external context.
raw_extraction.paper_title: Galactica: A Large Language Model for Science

raw_extraction.innovation: Scaled-down version of Galactica demonstrating strong performance on scientific tasks despite significantly fewer parameters than PaLM-540B, showcasing efficiency of specialized scientific training.
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Curated corpus of 106 billion tokens including 48M papers (88B tokens), code (7B tokens), reference material (7B tokens), knowledge bases (2B tokens), filtered Common Crawl (1B tokens), prompts (0.4B tokens), and other sources (0.2B tokens)
raw_extraction.application: Scientific literature review, equation solving, citation prediction, drug discovery (SMILES), protein sequence annotation, medical Q&A (PubMedQA, MedMCQA), and mathematical reasoning (MMLU)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Scalable scientific knowledge representation with reduced compute resources
raw_extraction.pretraining_task: Next-token prediction (with specialized tokens for citations, reasoning steps, SMILES, amino acids, DNA, LaTeX)
raw_extraction.extension: <work> token facilitates programmatic execution of complex calculations via embedded Python scripts during inference
raw_extraction.paper_title: Galactica: A Large Language Model for Science

raw_extraction.innovation: **External working memory token (`<work>`)** enables explicit step-by-step reasoning by offloading computations to Python scripts, while **prompt-inclusive pre-training** integrates task-specific prompts into the general corpus to enhance downstream task performance
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Curated scientific corpora including prompt datasets (e.g., OneSmallStep, KhanProblems, GSM8k, ChemicalProperties, Multiple-ChoiceQA, etc.)
raw_extraction.application: Scientific question answering, mathematical reasoning, chemical/physical equation solving, biomedical knowledge retrieval, and domain-specific QA tasks
raw_extraction.license: Closed source (no explicit license mentioned; proprietary)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Enabling large language models to handle scientific reasoning, symbolic computation, and domain-specific knowledge extraction
raw_extraction.pretraining_task: Causal language modeling with prompt-inclusive pre-training
raw_extraction.extension: <work> token facilitates programmatic execution of complex calculations via embedded Python scripts during inference
raw_extraction.paper_title: Galactica: A Large Language Model for Science

raw_extraction.application: Scientific question answering, mathematical reasoning, chemical/physical equation solving, biomedical knowledge retrieval, and domain-specific QA tasks

raw_extraction.extension: <work> token facilitates programmatic execution of complex calculations via embedded Python scripts during inference

raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Curated scientific corpora including prompt datasets (e.g., OneSmallStep, KhanProblems, GSM8k, ChemicalProperties, Multiple-ChoiceQA, etc.)

raw_extraction.paper_title: Galactica: A Large Language Model for Science

raw_extraction.innovation: Galactica is a specialized large language model designed specifically for scientific domains using a massive corpus of scientific literature, reference material, and structured knowledge bases. It incorporates citation processing, mathematical content recovery, and domain-specific prompt engineering.
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: 48 million scientific papers (arXiv, PMC, Semantic Scholar, PubMed Abstracts, bioRxiv, OSF, medRxiv, ACL, PubAg Abstracts, ChemRxiv) + 8 million reference materials (Wikipedia, StackExchange, LibreText, Wikibooks, OpenTextbooks, MIT OCW, Wikiversity, ProofWiki, Khan Academy, PapersWithCode, IUPAC Goldbook) + scientific knowledge bases (PubChem Compound, UniProt, RefSeq Genome, OEIS, Ribosome, LIPIDMAPS, Reactome)
raw_extraction.application: Scientific question answering, literature review, hypothesis generation, data interpretation, chemical/molecular structure reasoning, biological pathway analysis, and domain-specific code generation.
raw_extraction.license: Closed source (restricted access via Meta’s evaluation platform)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Creating a large language model capable of understanding and generating scientific content across disciplines including biology, chemistry, physics, medicine, and computer science.
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Instruction tuning and domain-specific fine-tuning for scientific tasks
raw_extraction.paper_title: Galactica: A Large Language Model for Science
